titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Pierpont Family Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by John Espy and Lynn J. Stewart

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday March 13, 2020 at 07:02
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: Pierpont Family Papers
did.abstract: Correspondence, diaries, financial and legal papers, inventories, deeds, and accounts of members of the Pierpont family of North Haven, Connecticut. The principal figure is Daniel Pierpont, business man and town clerk of North Haven whose papers include an account book kept while building a house (1846), fourteen deeds for land in North Haven and a description of a churn invented by him. His diary (1824) contains an account of a murder trial in New Haven interrupted by the arrival of General Lafayette, and a description of a journey through southern New York state where he observed preparations for the reception of LaFayette. There is also an estate inventory for Hezekiah Pierpont (1792), financial papers of Rufus Pierpont and a resolution (Aug 1, 1855) of St. John's Church on his death.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Pierpont Family Papers (MS 1125). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Mrs. E. Nesbit, 1949; gift of Mrs. Jordon T. Jack and Mrs. Ralph A. Winter, 1982.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Papers relating to the genealogy of the Pierpont family are available on microfilm (97 frames on 1 reel, 35mm.) from Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library, at cost. Order no. HM144.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Pierpont's Store Collection, MS Group No. 399.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers of the Pierpont Family of North Haven, Conn., chiefly of Daniel Pierpont, 1775-1851, a business man and town clerk there for many years.

Daniel Pierpont's diary, Jan.-Aug. 1824, giving an account of a murder trial in New Haven which was interrupted by the arrival of General Lafayette on Aug. 21, 1824. Included also is a journal [inversed and reversed] of a journey, Sep. 2-18, 1824, with William Warner. In the course of the journey they traveled from North Haven to Oxford, N. Y., though southern New York state and returned by a different route; passing through Cairo, N. Y. where they observed throngs of people preparing for Lafayette's arrival.

Two letters, one a draft of a letter, from Daniel Pierpont to an unnamed person concerning the writer's claim to some land apparently in New York state in the vicinity of Montrose [noted as mailed Sep. 12, 1832] and the other, Dec. 30, 1841 is from Dennis Kimberly to Daniel Pierpont.

Fourteen deeds, 1806-1865, all to Daniel Pierpont and to land in North Haven, unless otherwise indicated, from the following: Jesse Andrews, May 2, 1811; Elbridge Gerry Blanchard, July 24, 1837 [also to Elias Pierpont]; Moses. T. Brockett, Nov. 6, 1820; Jonathan H. Dayton, Aug. 6, 1836; John Gill, May 17, 1830 [in Susquehanna County, Penn.]; Town of Hamden, Conn., Dec. 25, 1844 [signed by Leverett Hitchcock, treas.]; Albert E. Moulthrop, Nov. 5, 1845; Elias Pierpont, 1803-1883, to his sister, Harriet Pierpont 1808- ,June 10, 1856; Harriet R. Pierpont to Elias and Harriet Pierpont, June 8, 1865; Ruel Todd, [with his wife, Lucinda (Pierpont) Todd], Jan. 1, 1828 [in Hamden]; William Todd. Jr. Sep. 14, 1836 [a building in North Haven]; Isaac Tuttle, June 4, 9 [in Hamden], 1842; Edward Whedon, June 18, 1806 [in Branford].

The miscellaneous papers consist of 15 items, 1792-1885, and undated. They include: inventory, Sep. 10, 1792, of Land and livestock, labeled "Hezekiah Pierpont his Bill of Estate", indenture, Dec. 10, 1798, from Joshua Barnes and James Ives, binding Chancy Linds to Isaiah Brocket; selectior by the Proprietors of North Haven, at meeting Nov. 30. 1807, Daniel Pierpont clerk, of a Committee to petition the General Assemble at Hartford for permission to build a dyke across the New Haven East River; list, Aug. 29, 1809, by Daniel Pierpont, of stock tools and fine woods (mahogany, ect.), endorsed "When we first commenced business"; agreement, June 10, 1824, in the hand of Daniel Pierpont, between him, of the one part, and his brother, Ezra (1757-1842), Joseph (1760-1833), and Russell (1763-1844) Pierpont of the other part concerning the estate of their father Joseph Pierpont, 1730-1824, deposition, Aug. 21, 1828, of Abner Strong, in the case of the town of Canton vs. North Haven cause not specified [mutilated]; list of qualified electors chosen at a meeting of the selectmen of North Haven, Mar. 28, 1842; Daniel Pierpont's "Building Acts," being his record, Apr.-Sep. 1846, of materials, workmen, etc. on a house he was building with entries on weather and other matters [1 pocket account book]; bill, June 3, 1841 from Charles Durand to [Rufus Pierpont, 1818-1855] for dry goods; bill Sep. 20, 1847, from E. E. Huggins & Co. to Rufus Pierpont, for chinaware; resolutions; Aug. 1, 1855, of St. John's Church on the death of Rufus Pierpont; copy of writ and original complaint from the Justice's court, New Haven, June 18, 1885, by Edward L. Linsly, J. P. concerning the case of Whitney Elliott vs. Mrs. Harriet R. Pierpont, Elijah M. Beaumont and Gustavus Eliot; description of a churn invented by Daniel Pierpont, [undated]; list with valuations of his livestock and real estate [undated]; his Division of Debts etc. added & cast on his paper being one folder of figures with names and amounts and nature of business not indicated.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

persname.part: Lafayette, Marie Joseph Paul Yves Roch Gilbert Du Motier, marquis de, 1757-1834

did.unittitle: Inventory
General family papers
Deeds
Legal papers
Hezekiah Pierpont
Estate inventory
Daniel Pierpont
Correspondence
Diary and journal
Description of "Pierpont's Churn"
Account book, with accounts for the building of a house
Inventories and accounts
Rufus Pierpont
Resolutions of St. John's Church, North Haven, on his death
Bills
Additional material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Preliminary inventory by Lynn J. Stewart 1982 Aug
Received from: Mrs. Jordan T. Jack and Mrs. Ralph A. Winter, 1982 Jul 14

did.unittitle: Correspondence
ALS John Eaton to John Pierpont and a copy (reverse is extract of letter from Thomas Hill to John Pierpont, 1664 Apr 15)
ALS Thomas Hill to John Pierpont
ALS Jeremiah Dummer, agent for Connecticut, in England, to Rev. James Pierpont
ALS Jeremiah Dummer to Rev. James Pierpont
ALS Sir Richard Waller to Rev. James Pierpont
ALS Rev. James Pierpont to Sir Richard Waller
ALS Jeremiah Dummer to Rev. James Pierpont
ALS James Pierpont, Y. 1718 (son of Rev. James Pierpont) to Jeremiah Dummer (fragment)
Jeremiah Dummer to James Pierpont, Y. 1718
Drafts of letter from James Pierpont to the duke of Kingston, (one written on reverse of letter from Sam Royall to James Pierpont, 1725 Nov 13)
ALS Governor Talcott to James Pierpont
Copy of James Pierpont to Henry Newman with copy of enclosure from James Pierpont to the duke of Kingston
Copy of letter from James Pierpont to Evelyn, 2nd duke of Kingston
ALS James Pierpont to Eliakim Palmer, with a copy of James Pierpont to the duke of Kingston
Copy of James Pierpont to Eliakim Palmer with postscript to friends in Boston
ALS Eliakim Palmer, agent for Connecticut in London, to James Pierpont
ALS James Pierpont to the successor of E. Palmer as agent for Connecticut in London
Extract of a letter from Dr. Gideon Wells to Rev. Partridge, re James Pierpont.
ALS James Pierpont to the president of Dartmouth College, re relationship to duke of Kingston
2 copies of that letter, one incomplete
ALS Robert Pierpont, Jr. to Evelyn Pierpont and a copy of that letter
ALS Thomas Clarke to Hezekiah B. Pierpont, enclosing copies of birth and baptismal records for Rev. James Pierpont
Genealogy

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 144 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Deposition by Sarah Pierpont and by Shubel Seaver, John May and John Mayo
Deposition by Sarah Pierpont
Certified copy of birth, marriage, death and baptismal records for family of Rev. James Pierpont
Certified copies of birth and baptismal records for family of James Pierpont
Indenture to finance Evelyn Pierpont's claim to the title and estate of the duke of Kingston
